entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 31 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-31 19:27PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Commonwealth Games 2022 Day 3 LIVE updates: McKeon wins record 11th gold; Horror crash leaves cyclists in hospital; Australia win cycling and gymnastics gold
  • Lionesses beat Germany to win Euro 2022 and shatter England’s 1966 hoodoo
  • ASX set to jump as Wall Street closes bumper July with rally
  • Game on: Bids roll in for multi-billion dollar AFL deal
  • Using your gut to fix your brain: We’re not quite there with psychobiotics just yet
  • Dom is only 29, but he’s making plans for a ‘good death’
  • Future of all statues should be regularly assessed, historian says
  • Sky-high fuel prices blow out cost of Perth commuters’ daily drive
  • Sydney’s mega rail projects running into ‘high’ safety, financial risks
  • ‘Supermarkets will do well’ as grocery prices rise
  • ‘Whatever you do, make it count’: Kerry O’Brien’s advice to Stan Grant
  • As the PM spoke at Garma, I had tears in my eyes
  • Failure to spend bushfire donations prompts WIRES board defection
  • Mark Ryan exits embattled journalism institute
  • Teachers to get helping hand preparing for lessons
  • ‘It will be really positive’: ABC news boss to work in Parramatta when staff relocate
  • ‘Totally out of scale’: Towers set to rise above 100 metres in Pyrmont
  • Union head demands default membership for skilled migrants
  • Veil lifted on Barilaro papers, pressure mounts on Ayres to step aside
  • AFL round 20 key takeouts and match review news
  • Spender calls for tax reform while delivering a warning to the Coalition
  • Parliament to host de facto euthanasia debate with territory rights bill
  • House values tumble and could drop faster as rates set to rise
  • Bipartisan support for Labor’s climate plans crucial, green groups and industry say
  • Best of cartoons, August 1, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Best of cartoons, July 31, 2022
  • Detail of Indigenous Voice to come after referendum: Albanese
  • Border closures an ‘overly simplistic’ solution to foot and mouth risk, industry says
  • A sweet parliamentary week, but real change can be a drag – just ask the Speaker
  • Enact Voice so we never again overlook Indigenous
  • In the Herald: August 1, 1936
  • Suburban soldiers on alert
  • ‘The power players are right in your face’: How David Chandler took on Sydney’s shoddy developers
  • From racing for gold to saving lives: Pittman trades track for medicine
  • Fidel Ramos, ex-Philippine leader who helped oust dictator, dies
  • ‘There is no other way’: Judges on hunger strike in fight for Tunisian democracy
  • Massive wave of Iraqi protesters storm into Green Zone, occupy parliament
  • Zelensky announces mandatory evacuation of Donetsk region
  • US President Joe Biden tests positive for COVID-19 in rare ‘rebound’ case
  • Diner accidentally discovers dinosaur footprints at Chinese restaurant
  • Women better in driverless cars than men
  • Prince Charles ‘accepted millions’ from family of Osama bin Laden
  • James Packer’s $250,000 gift to free Julian Assange
  • Listing breakthrough brings hope for people with spinal condition
  • From the Archives, 1962: A fighting general who hated ‘blimps’
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Monday, August 1
  • Shrine lights for LGBTQ community inappropriate, RSL says
  • The inflation fix: protect profits, hit workers and consumers
  • ‘Operation Blue Sky’: Inside Zip’s fight for survival
  • RBA rate rises tipped to slow bank mortgage growth
  • ‘We asked questions about it’: Small companies warned by regulator of flimsy net-zero claims
  • It’s Gillard v Bishop in a bizarre, clever and funny satirical showdown
  • Ione Skye on her wild years with Anthony Kiedis and her marriage to Ben Lee
  • ‘Miracle of a film’: The surprising masterpiece you’ll only see at MIFF
  • ‘We’re all scared’: Ewen Leslie on bad guys and why public opinion is the true monster
  • Studio boss who brought Thor to Sydney will run Luna Park
  • New film is a love song to a city that was almost lost to the wrecking ball
  • Good news on climate is so rare, it should be celebrated
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Former Philippine president Fidel Ramos dies at 94
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Putin trumpets new hypersonic missiles; Russian Navy HQ hit in drone attack
  • Pelosi announces Asia trip itinerary with no mention of Taiwan
  • China’s most powerful rocket falls back to Earth, lands in criticism
  • Why dangerous content thrives on Facebook and TikTok in Kenya
  • Guatemalan journalist arrested in growing crackdown on political dissent
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia accused of ‘deliberate mass murder’; Donetsk ordered to evacuate
  • Russia wants Viktor Bout back, badly. The question is: Why?
  • Part of Beirut port silos, damaged in 2020 blast, collapses
  • Drone explosion hits Russia's Black Sea Fleet headquarters
  • Briton leaves Iraq after antiquities conviction quashed
  • Iran says border guards have clashed with Afghan Taliban
  • Red Cross: 10 killed in flooding in hilly eastern Uganda
  • Iraq cleric's followers camped out in parliament for 2nd day
  • Ramos, ex-Philippine leader who helped oust dictator, dies
  • South Africa's ANC says economy, corruption are priorities
  • Ukraine war hangs over UN meeting on nukes treaty's legacy
  • Report: Prince Charles' charity got donation from bin Ladens
  • Nichelle Nichols, who played Uhura in ‘Star Trek’ franchise, dies at 89
  • Death toll for Kentucky floods climbs to 26, with more storms coming
  • An Oklahoma city’s first openly gay mayor resigned. Then came the fallout.
  • Fires engulf western Nebraska wildlife area, sparking evacuations
  • Young guns
  • Okla. downgrades school district over complaint it shamed White people
  • At least 25 dead in Kentucky in devastating flood, governor says
  • Four indicted in robbery of mailbox keys, stolen-check scheme
  • The Claremont Institute triumphed in the Trump years. Then came Jan. 6.
  • A policy win, an economic hit: Turbulent week reflects Biden’s challenge
  • Biden tests positive again for coronavirus after Paxlovid ‘rebound’
  • Thousands of lives depend on a transplant network in need of ‘vast restructuring’
  • Democrats Need More Joe Manchins
  • The Free Market Responds to America’s School Shootings
  • Can the US Housing Crisis Be Fixed By Abolishing Zoning?
  • Women’s Wealth Is Growing. When Will Finance Catch Up?
  • Why the Euro Has Tumbled to Parity Against the Dollar
  • Indiana’s cruel abortion bill is a warning of post-Roe reality
  • In a normal year, the GOP should sweep. But 2022 isn’t normal.
  • Six million died. We still don’t know how the pandemic began.
  • Dark days again in Russia. For prisoners, an endless carousel of absurdity.
  • Air travel is in chaos — and there are no easy solutions
  • Want to know what a ‘banana republic’ looks like?
  • Cartoon by Drew Sheneman
  • A sinister plan
  • We face not a climate emergency but a climate catastrophe
  • Democrats should stop meddling in Republican primaries
  • The ending of Latin Mass as a symbol
  • The benefits they seek are not universal
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Women’s Euro 2022 Golden Boot: top goalscorers, game by game

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 31 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-31 18:08PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Snoozing Hong Kong police officer faces disciplinary action after service gun left unattended
  • Coronavirus: no special quarantine deal for critically hurt Mirror dancer’s parents, Hong Kong officials say, with nearly 1,700 applications approved to leave isolation

  • Hong Kong’s urban renewal chief rejects calls to help Housing Authority develop public flats

  • Will sharp rises in interest rates diminish Hong Kong’s love affair with property?
  • Hong Kong protests: Oscar-winning director Malcolm Clarke releases documentary series offering different take on 2019 unrest, says he is not ‘working for the Chinese government’
  • Hong Kong’s Star Ferry on the brink of closure? No way, says company
  • Tiger Global-backed Hong Kong start-up SleekFlow targets US$300 million valuation as social e-commerce grows

  • China’s cyberspace watchdog reports ‘significant results’ in online platform regulation
  • Xi Jinping urges Chinese Communist Party to step up efforts to ‘win hearts and minds’ in Hong Kong and Taiwan

  • New York-based Penetron develops concrete solutions to reduce carbon footprint of buildings and infrastructure in Hong Kong and China
  • China posts shock contraction in factory activity for July despite economic rebound

  • China, Japan to hold in-person foreign ministerial talks on sidelines of Asean meeting in Cambodia next week: sources

  • Vietnam robber-cop arrested after offering passers-by gold and jewellery he stole in raid on shops
  • Philippine ex-president Fidel Ramos, who helped end Marcos dictatorship, dies at 94
  • Japanese national detained in Myanmar, embassy confirms
  • Pakistan’s young people rue 75 years of power struggles in a country ‘no one wants to own’
  • Youth in India have found a new way to get high; flavoured condoms
  • Indian travellers hoping to escape summer heat face passport, visa woes
  • Why Asia-Pacific governments should let independent news thrive
  • In Thailand, having a regular job could still leave you US$218,000 in debt
  • US-China relations: Asean needs to pay attention to great power rivalry in the Pacific
  • Malaysia’s Tasik Chini risks being stripped of its Unesco status unless decades of damage to ‘dragon god lake’ is undone
  • Israel’s prime minister vows investigation into ‘sex slave’ prison case
  • British geologist jailed in Iraq for smuggling antiquities freed after conviction quashed
  • Putin says Russian Navy will soon have hypersonic missiles five times faster than the speed of sound
  • Commonwealth Games: Horrifying crash injures cyclists and spectators
  • Ukrainian grain tycoon killed as first export ship prepares to leave Odesa

  • Ukraine war: Russia says it has invited UN, Red Cross experts to probe jail deaths
  • Ukraine war: Lebanon seizes ship carrying ‘stolen’ Ukrainian grain
  • Italy shocked by video of fatal attack on African immigrant
  • Unvaccinated Novak Djokovic still hopes to play at US Open
  • Zelensky orders mandatory evacuations in Ukraine’s Donetsk region as fighting intensifies
  • Signs of US economic slowdown complicate Federal Reserve’s inflation battle
  • Chinese regulator says shadow banking sector needs constant scrutiny to guard against risks spilling over into the economy
  • Another global crash on the horizon as the world’s economic engines sputter
  • Disney+ K-drama Adamas: Ji Sung plays double roles in stylish high-society mystery
  • One of the most exciting literary talents, Chinese-American Meng Jin, burnishes her reputation with short story collection
  • Doctors recommend 2 years of breastfeeding, but other experts stress that whether to do it, and for how long, is always a personal choice
  • Indonesia has been called ‘the world’s most invisible country’, but look beyond Bali and Java and you’ll find a wealth of traditional culture and architecture
  • Dad ‘doused me in kerosene’: Christina Chung on her abusive father, rebellious daughters and her return to modelling at 50
  • ‘It’s not a portrait of me’: Pipilotti Rist, Swiss video artist, on Behind Your Eyelid, her Hong Kong show
  • From the Aztecs to avo, the scoop on ‘avocado’ and ‘guacamole’ – its tropical American origins and how alligator pear entered the mix
  • In war on cancer, new drugs and therapies suggest we are on our way to finding cures
  • Why food portions are getting smaller: ‘shrinkflation’ at restaurants means less on your plate for the same price
  • A change from Jackie Chan and Jet Li – Beast Cops’ Gordon Chan on how directing the Hong Kong gangs vs police film was not like his previous work
  • 5 stars who made a comeback after retiring: from Cameron Diaz announcing a new Netflix film and Tom Brady returning to football after just 40 days, to Jay-Z being ‘terrible’ at retirement
  • Meet Formula One heiress Tamara Ecclestone, daughter of billionaire Bernie Ecclestone: she’s offering millions for her stolen jewellery – and lives in a mansion near Prince William and Kate Middleton
  • Inside luxury fashion’s sustainability evolution: Gucci, Louis Vuitton and Dior are offering eco-friendly designs, with celebrities from Billie Eilish to Emma Stone voicing their support
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • How the Kremlin Is Forcing Ukrainians to Adopt Russian Life
  • Trained, Armed and Ready. To Teach Kindergarten.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • Japan delegation says support resolute
  • Defense budget to grow 4% on China threats: sources
